description: Acquisition of Baring Private Equity Asia (BPEA) for approximately $7.5 billion in cash and EQT shares, creating the largest pan-Asian private equity platform under a European alt manager. BPEA managed approximately $23B in AUM across PE, real estate, and special situations across 8 Asian markets.
stated_rationale: Accelerate geographic diversification into Asia-Pacific, the fastest-growing private markets region. Combine EQT's European and US PE franchise with BPEA's deep Asian origination network to create a globally integrated investment platform. Expected to add ~EUR 2B in annual fee revenue at maturity.
observable_metric_impact: AUM increased by approximately $23B upon closing. Added 200+ investment professionals across Asia. Expanded geographic footprint to include India, Greater China, Southeast Asia, and Australia. Fee-paying AUM uplift of ~EUR 12B at close.
op_prereq_summary: Required regulatory approvals across multiple Asian jurisdictions (SFC Hong Kong, MAS Singapore, SEBI India, ACCC Australia). Involved integration of distinct investment cultures and team retention across 8 country offices.

description: Acquisition of EQT Exeter (formerly Exeter Property Group) in 2021 for approximately $1.9 billion, establishing EQT's dedicated real estate vertical. EQT Exeter manages industrial, logistics, and office assets across the US and Europe with approximately $14B in AUM at time of acquisition.
stated_rationale: Enter the real estate asset class at scale rather than building organically. Exeter's US industrial/logistics specialization complemented EQT's European platform. Real estate represents a high-AUM, recurring-fee asset class that diversifies the revenue base beyond PE and infrastructure.
observable_metric_impact: Added approximately $14B in real estate AUM at close. EQT Exeter has since grown to approximately $31B in AUM by mid-2025. Provides exposure to US industrial/logistics sector which benefited from post-pandemic supply chain reconfiguration. Real estate now represents roughly 11% of total EQT AUM.
op_prereq_summary: Required integration of Exeter's US-centric real estate operations into EQT's European-headquartered platform. Key-man risk managed through long-term retention arrangements for Exeter founder and senior team.

description: Final close of EQT Infrastructure VI at approximately EUR 21 billion, making it the largest infrastructure fund ever raised globally at the time of closing. The fund targets value-add infrastructure investments across digital infrastructure, energy transition, transportation, and social infrastructure in Europe and North America.
stated_rationale: Cement EQT's position as a leading global infrastructure investor. Scale benefits in infrastructure include improved sourcing access, platform-level operational improvements across portfolio companies, and bargaining leverage in large-cap transactions that smaller competitors cannot access.
observable_metric_impact: Exceeded EUR 20B hard cap by approximately EUR 1B. Surpassed predecessor fund (Infra V, EUR 15.7B) by ~34%. Flagship infrastructure series now represents the largest single strategy within EQT by fee-paying AUM. Anchored long-duration management fee base (~12-year fund life).
op_prereq_summary: Required global LP roadshow spanning sovereign wealth funds, pension systems, and insurance companies. Deployment pace of predecessor fund (Infra V) was a key marketing proof point. EU SFDR Article 8 classification required sustainability disclosures at fund level.

description: Launch of EQT Active Core Infrastructure (EQT Nexus), a semi-liquid open-ended fund targeting core-plus infrastructure investments. The strategy provides long-duration exposure to operational infrastructure assets with periodic liquidity windows, designed for private wealth and institutional investors seeking alternatives to traditional drawdown fund structures.
stated_rationale: Access the growing private-wealth distribution channel by offering a liquidity profile compatible with wealth management platforms. Complement EQT's existing value-add drawdown infrastructure funds with a permanent-capital vehicle that generates perpetual management fees without fundraising cycles. Respond to LP demand for infrastructure exposure with lower risk and income orientation.
observable_metric_impact: Initial fundraising of approximately EUR 3-4 billion within first 18 months of launch. Opened EQT's infrastructure franchise to wealth management platforms and private bank distribution networks. Contributes to perpetual capital base, reducing dependence on cyclical drawdown fundraising.
op_prereq_summary: Required buildout of dedicated private-wealth distribution team and operational infrastructure for periodic liquidity (NAV calculation, redemption processing). Distinct from existing drawdown funds in governance, fee structure, and portfolio construction approach.
